title: salvia rosmarinus subsp. palaui
intro: Salvia rosmarinus subsp. palaui, also known as the Palaui rosemary, is a subspecies of the rosemary plant (Salvia rosmarinus) native to the Canary Islands. It is a fragrant, evergreen shrub with needle-like leaves that have been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Palaui rosemary include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and supporting respiratory health, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and skin care. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
